Description: Hypertension is a chronic condition in which blood pressure remains consistently elevated, increasing the risk of cardiovascular disease.
Causes: Genetics, high salt intake, obesity, lack of exercise, kidney disease, hormonal disorders
Risk_Factors: Age over 40, family history, obesity, diabetes, smoking, high sodium diet
Symptoms: Headache, dizziness, blurred vision, chest pain, shortness of breath, nosebleeds
Emergency_Signs: Hypertensive emergency, severe chest pain, confusion, vision loss, difficulty breathing
Diagnosis: Repeated blood pressure measurements, physical examination, blood tests, ECG, kidney function tests
Treatment: Lifestyle modification, low-salt diet, regular exercise, weight loss, antihypertensive medications
Complications: Heart attack, stroke, heart failure, kidney disease, vision loss
Prevention: Healthy diet, regular exercise, maintaining healthy weight, avoiding tobacco, limiting alcohol
References: WHO Hypertension Guideline 2021; NICE Hypertension Guideline 2019

Description: Diabetes mellitus is a chronic metabolic disorder characterized by elevated blood glucose levels due to insufficient insulin production, insulin resistance, or both.
Causes: Autoimmune destruction of pancreatic beta cells, insulin resistance, obesity, genetic factors
Risk_Factors: Family history, obesity, physical inactivity, unhealthy diet, age over 45, hypertension
Symptoms: Frequent urination, excessive thirst, increased hunger, fatigue, blurred vision, slow wound healing
Emergency_Signs: Diabetic ketoacidosis, severe hypoglycemia, altered consciousness, rapid breathing
Diagnosis: Fasting blood glucose, HbA1c, oral glucose tolerance test, random blood glucose
Treatment: Healthy diet, regular exercise, weight management, oral antidiabetic drugs, insulin therapy
Complications: Heart disease, kidney disease, nerve damage, diabetic retinopathy, foot ulcers
Prevention: Healthy lifestyle, balanced diet, regular exercise, maintaining healthy weight
References: ADA Standards of Care 2026; WHO SEARO Diabetes Protocol 2020

Description: Stroke is a medical emergency caused by interruption of blood supply to the brain or bleeding within the brain, leading to brain cell damage.
Causes: Blocked artery (ischemic stroke), ruptured blood vessel (hemorrhagic stroke), blood clots
Risk_Factors: Hypertension, diabetes, smoking, obesity, high cholesterol, atrial fibrillation, advanced age
Symptoms: Sudden weakness or numbness on one side, facial drooping, difficulty speaking, vision problems, dizziness, severe headache
Emergency_Signs: Sudden paralysis, loss of consciousness, seizures, difficulty breathing, severe headache
Diagnosis: Neurological examination, CT scan, MRI, blood tests, ECG, carotid ultrasound
Treatment: Immediate emergency care, thrombolytic therapy, thrombectomy (when eligible), blood pressure control, rehabilitation
Complications: Permanent disability, paralysis, speech impairment, swallowing problems, recurrent stroke, death
Prevention: Control blood pressure, diabetes and cholesterol, quit smoking, regular exercise, healthy diet

Description: Epilepsy is a chronic neurological disorder characterized by recurrent, unprovoked seizures caused by abnormal electrical activity in the brain.
Causes: Genetic factors, brain injury, stroke, brain infections, congenital abnormalities, brain tumors
Risk_Factors: Family history, head injury, stroke, brain infections, developmental disorders
Symptoms: Recurrent seizures, temporary confusion, staring spells, muscle stiffness, loss of consciousness, involuntary jerking movements
Emergency_Signs: Seizures lasting more than 5 minutes, repeated seizures without recovery, breathing difficulty, severe injury during seizure
Diagnosis: Medical history, neurological examination, EEG, MRI, CT scan, blood tests
Treatment: Anti-seizure medications, ketogenic diet in selected patients, vagus nerve stimulation, epilepsy surgery for selected cases
Complications: Status epilepticus, injuries, cognitive impairment, depression, sudden unexpected death in epilepsy (SUDEP)
Prevention: Take medications regularly, avoid seizure triggers, adequate sleep, avoid alcohol misuse, regular follow-up

Description: Tuberculosis (TB) is a contagious bacterial infection caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, primarily affecting the lungs but capable of involving other organs.
Causes: Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection transmitted through airborne droplets
Risk_Factors: Close contact with infected individuals, HIV infection, diabetes, malnutrition, smoking, weakened immune system
Symptoms: Persistent cough for more than two weeks, fever, night sweats, weight loss, fatigue, chest pain, coughing up blood
Emergency_Signs: Severe breathing difficulty, massive coughing of blood, altered consciousness, respiratory failure
Diagnosis: Chest X-ray, sputum smear microscopy, GeneXpert MTB/RIF, culture, tuberculin skin test
Treatment: Combination anti-tuberculosis therapy (HRZE regimen), nutritional support, regular follow-up
Complications: Drug-resistant TB, lung damage, meningitis, pleural effusion, death if untreated
Prevention: BCG vaccination, early diagnosis and treatment, infection control measures, good ventilation

Description: Eczema, also known as atopic dermatitis, is a chronic inflammatory skin condition that causes itchy, dry, and inflamed skin.
Causes: Genetic predisposition, immune system dysfunction, environmental triggers, allergens
Risk_Factors: Family history of eczema, asthma, allergies, dry skin, exposure to irritants
Symptoms: Itching, dry skin, redness, skin rash, cracked skin, swelling
Emergency_Signs: Severe skin infection, widespread rash with fever, intense swelling, difficulty breathing due to allergic reaction
Diagnosis: Clinical examination, medical history, allergy testing if required
Treatment: Regular use of moisturizers, topical corticosteroids, antihistamines, avoiding triggers
Complications: Skin infections, sleep disturbances, chronic skin thickening
Prevention: Daily moisturizing, avoiding irritants, gentle skin care, managing allergies

Description: Skin diseases are conditions that affect the skin, hair, or nails and may be caused by infections, inflammation, autoimmune disorders, allergies, or environmental factors.
Causes: Bacterial, viral or fungal infections, autoimmune disorders, allergic reactions, genetic conditions, environmental exposure
Risk_Factors: Poor hygiene, allergies, weakened immune system, family history, excessive sun exposure
Symptoms: Rashes, itching, redness, swelling, dry skin, blisters, scaling, skin discoloration
Emergency_Signs: Rapidly spreading rash, severe skin infection, high fever, extensive blistering, difficulty breathing due to allergic reaction
Diagnosis: Clinical examination, skin biopsy, laboratory tests, allergy testing, dermoscopy
Treatment: Topical medications, oral medicines, antibiotics or antifungals when indicated, phototherapy, skin care
Complications: Secondary infections, scarring, chronic inflammation, pigmentation changes
Prevention: Good hygiene, skin protection, avoiding allergens, sun protection, early treatment

Description: Emergency medical care involves the immediate assessment and treatment of life-threatening illnesses and injuries to stabilize patients and prevent further complications.
Causes: Trauma, cardiac arrest, stroke, severe infections, poisoning, accidents, burns
Risk_Factors: Road traffic accidents, chronic diseases, advanced age, delayed medical care, occupational hazards
Symptoms: Severe chest pain, difficulty breathing, uncontrolled bleeding, unconsciousness, severe burns, seizures
Emergency_Signs: Cardiac arrest, respiratory failure, severe bleeding, shock, unconsciousness, anaphylaxis
Diagnosis: Primary survey (ABCDE), physical examination, ECG, imaging, laboratory tests, vital signs assessment
Treatment: Immediate stabilization, airway management, oxygen therapy, IV fluids, emergency medications, surgery if required
Complications: Organ failure, permanent disability, infection, death
Prevention: Road safety, early recognition of emergencies, first aid training, preventive healthcare
References: WHO Emergency Triage Guideline 2023; NICE Emergency Care Guideline 2018

Description: COPD is a progressive lung disease characterized by persistent airflow limitation, most commonly caused by long-term exposure to harmful particles or gases.
Causes: Cigarette smoking, air pollution, occupational dust, biomass fuel exposure, genetic factors
Risk_Factors: Smoking, exposure to pollutants, older age, alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency
Symptoms: Chronic cough, sputum production, shortness of breath, wheezing, chest tightness
Emergency_Signs: Severe breathlessness, bluish lips, confusion, inability to speak due to breathlessness
Diagnosis: Spirometry, chest X-ray, CT scan, arterial blood gas analysis
Treatment: Smoking cessation, bronchodilators, inhaled corticosteroids, pulmonary rehabilitation, oxygen therapy
Complications: Respiratory failure, pulmonary hypertension, recurrent infections, heart disease
Prevention: Avoid smoking, reduce pollution exposure, vaccinations, regular exercise

Description: Asthma is a chronic inflammatory disease of the airways that causes recurrent episodes of wheezing, breathlessness, chest tightness, and coughing.
Causes: Genetic predisposition, allergies, respiratory infections, environmental triggers
Risk_Factors: Family history, allergies, obesity, smoking exposure, air pollution
Symptoms: Wheezing, cough, shortness of breath, chest tightness, nighttime coughing
Emergency_Signs: Severe asthma attack, inability to speak, bluish lips, confusion, respiratory distress
Diagnosis: Medical history, physical examination, spirometry, peak flow measurement
Treatment: Inhaled bronchodilators, inhaled corticosteroids, avoiding triggers, asthma action plan
Complications: Respiratory failure, frequent hospitalizations, reduced quality of life
Prevention: Avoid allergens, avoid smoking, vaccination, regular follow-up
References: Iraq Ministry of Health Asthma Guideline 2013
